summary: Ukrainian forces forced Russian occupiers to retreat from positions on the Kinburn Spit after heavy fire. Russia launched a night Iskander‑M ballistic missile strike and 90 drones across multiple regions, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian attacks hit the Russian‑controlled oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban for the second time this month.
key_points: Ukrainian Southern Defense Forces inflicted heavy fire on the Kinburn Spit, forcing Russian occupiers to withdraw and evacuate surviving personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/82170).
Russia struck Sumy region with an Iskander‑M ballistic missile and 90 drones, injuring four civilians and damaging a gas station in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Five civilians were injured in Bogodukhov, Kharkiv region, after a drone hit a building, igniting a garage and three cars (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
The Russian‑controlled oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban was hit again, with at least three fuel tanks burning (Source: @DeepStateUA/23593).
Multiple ballistic missiles and over 20 Shahed drones were launched at Kremenchuk and its power facilities, including attacks on the Kremenchuk HPP and TEC (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
Ukrainian forces reported a single Shahed drone tracking toward the Kremenchuk hydro‑electric plant (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68072).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the eastern and south‑eastern fronts, raising the threat of air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/66387; @kpszsu/66392).
Belarusian repeater stations allegedly used by Russia for drone guidance are reported non‑operational, according to Ukrainian officials (Source: @UkraineNow/82176).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces forced Russian occupiers to retreat from the Kinburn Spit after intense artillery fire, marking a notable tactical gain in the south. Russia responded with a coordinated night strike using an Iskander‑M missile and 90 drones, causing civilian casualties in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv regions. Ukrainian attacks also struck the Russian‑held oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban for the second time this month, igniting multiple fuel tanks.

points: Ukrainian Southern Defense reported that heavy fire compelled Russian occupiers to abandon positions on the Kinburn Spit, with evacuation of surviving enemy personnel underway (Source: @UkraineNow/82170).
Ukrainian air defenses downed numerous drones over Crimea and Sevastopol, as part of ongoing operations to protect the peninsula (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces claimed successful strikes on the Russian‑controlled oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban, resulting in three burning fuel tanks (Source: @DeepStateUA/23593).
Ukrainian authorities reported that a temporary power outage regime was imposed in occupied Crimea to prevent grid overloads (Source: @UkraineNow/82168).
Ukrainian command approved a 40‑day SBU operation aimed at influencing the aggressor state, as announced by President Zelensky (Source: @UkraineNow/82179).
Russian forces launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile at Sumy region during a night attack, accompanied by 90 drones, injuring four civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
A Russian drone strike hit a gas station in Zaporizhzhia, wounding one woman and producing visible smoke (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
In Bogodukhov, Kharkiv region, a Russian drone strike injured five people, damaged a building façade, an electrical line, and ignited a garage with three cars (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian tactical aviation activity was observed on the eastern and south‑eastern axes, indicating potential air strike threats to frontline zones (Source: @kpszsu/66387; @kpszsu/66392).
Russian‑linked UAV activity was reported near the Sumy‑Chernihiv border, moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/66363).
Russian tactical aviation was active on the eastern direction, heightening the risk of air strikes on frontline settlements (Source: @kpszsu/66387).
Ukrainian reports indicated a ballistic missile strike in Sumy region accompanied by 90 drones, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
A Shahed drone was tracked heading toward the Kremenchuk hydro‑electric plant, a key energy target in the east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68072).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border, moving southward, suggesting reconnaissance or strike preparation (Source: @kpszsu/66363).
No specific combat reports were found for the northern front in the provided messages.
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the eastern direction, which may affect northern sectors indirectly (Source: @kpszsu/66387).
Ukrainian defensive alerts mentioned potential missile threats from Kursk region, which could impact northern territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68075).
Ukrainian intelligence highlighted the non‑operation of Belarusian repeater stations used for Russian drone guidance, reducing northern electronic threat (Source: @UkraineNow/82176).
Ukrainian Southern Defense forced Russian occupiers to retreat from the Kinburn Spit after heavy fire, evacuating surviving enemy troops (Source: @UkraineNow/82170).
Multiple ballistic missiles and over 20 Shahed drones were launched at Kremenchuk, striking its hydro‑electric plant and thermal power station (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
Additional Shahed attacks targeted Kremenchuk HPP (2 drones) and TEC (1 drone) as reported by local monitors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68069).
Russian‑controlled oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban suffered a second strike, igniting three fuel tanks, affecting logistics for the southern front (Source: @DeepStateUA/23593).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down numerous drones over Crimea and Sevastopol during night operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian tactical aviation activity was observed on eastern and south‑eastern axes, indicating possible upcoming air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66387; @kpszsu/66392).
A Russian missile and drone barrage hit Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv regions, demonstrating continued Russian air‑to‑ground operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian forces reported a single Shahed drone tracking toward the Kremenchuk hydro‑electric plant, highlighting ongoing low‑altitude UAV threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68072).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces forced Russian troops to withdraw from the Kinburn Spit after intense artillery fire, evacuating surviving enemy personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/82170).
Russia launched an Iskander‑M missile and 90 drones in a night attack on Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv, injuring civilians and damaging infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian strikes hit the Russian‑held oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban for the second time this month, igniting three fuel tanks (Source: @DeepStateUA/23593).
Over 20 Shahed drones and multiple ballistic missiles attacked Kremenchuk, targeting its hydro‑electric plant and thermal power station (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on eastern and south‑eastern fronts, raising the threat of air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/66387; @kpszsu/66392).
Ukrainian air defenses downed drones over Crimea and Sevastopol, maintaining control of the peninsula's airspace (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Belarusian repeater stations allegedly used by Russia for drone guidance are reported non‑operational, reducing Russian electronic targeting capability (Source: @UkraineNow/82176).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone strikes continue to target civilian infrastructure in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv, indicating a sustained effort to disrupt logistics and morale. The concentration of Shahed drones and ballistic missiles on Kremenchuk's energy facilities threatens regional power stability and could hamper Ukrainian counter‑offensive operations in the south. Russian tactical aviation activity on eastern and south‑eastern axes suggests preparation for intensified air campaigns, while the disabling of Belarusian repeater stations may limit Russian drone guidance capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian Southern Defense successfully repelled Russian forces on the Kinburn Spit, demonstrating effective use of artillery and coordination. Ukrainian air defense units have increased interception rates, downing drones over Crimea and protecting key energy sites. International partners have highlighted the strategic importance of the Kuban oil depot, with Ukraine leveraging drone strikes to degrade Russian fuel logistics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed drone launch sites near Kremenchuk should be intensified to pre‑empt further attacks on energy infrastructure. Continued surveillance of Russian tactical aviation movements on the eastern front is required to anticipate potential air strikes. Verification of the operational status of Belarusian repeater stations will inform assessments of Russian drone targeting accuracy.
